symbols: boats on the water — the Church on its mission
Christ commanding — divine authority over nature
description: Raphael's famous cartoon (full-scale preparatory drawing) for a Sistine Chapel tapestry showing the Miraculous Draught of Fishes. The composition shows Christ in a boat commanding the disciples who haul in an enormous catch. The scene is rendered with monumental figures and dramatic composition designed for large-scale reproduction.
theological_description: The Miraculous Draught (LUK.5.1-11) combines divine provision with apostolic calling. After a fruitless night, Jesus commands: 'Put out into the deep and let down your nets' (LUK.5.4). The resulting catch overwhelms the boats. Peter falls at Jesus's knees: 'Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, O Lord' (LUK.5.8). Jesus responds: 'From now on you will be catching men' (LUK.5.10). The miracle establishes the apostolic mission: human effort empowered by divine command yields an abundance beyond calculation.

scripture_refs.reason: The miraculous catch and calling of the disciples
From now on you will be catching men
The post-Resurrection catch, echoing this scene

_meta.title: The Miraculous Draught of Fishes (cartoon for the Sistine Chapel)
